w:r.w:t: This section demonstrates how four Large Language Models (LLMs) —
information about the villa’s historical owners
artworks connected to these owners and to the villa
information about the villa’s botanical gardens
All retrieved data is later used for
“Provide a complete historical overview of the owners of Villa Carlotta (
, Lake Como). Include dates of ownership, historical context, and how each owner influenced the development of the villa.”
Gives detailed biographies and ownership dates.
Provides the richest historical context, including political background.
purchased it in 1801 and transformed it into a museum.
received it in 1843, giving the villa its modern name.
collection were located in Villa Carlotta.”
“Provide a complete list of artworks connected to Villa Carlotta through its owners.”
collection to the villa; identifies Canova and Thorvaldsen.
Adds historical details about exhibitions and provenance.
Gives a shorter but highly accurate list.
Some pieces remain in the villa’s museum today.
, and Villa Carlotta historically the same building? Provide historical evidence, ownership transitions, and naming chronology.”
Provides a chronological explanation of name changes.
Gives historical sources and contextual evidence.
3. Additional Missing Information Retrieved via LLMs
3.2 Artworks Connected to the Villa (Missing in
LLMs identify artworks linked to the villa through
3.3 Evidence That All Three Names Refer to One Villa
3.4 Artworks Depicting or Related to the Villa
century landscape paintings of Lake Como featuring the villa
Final Table: Missing Information Found by LLMs
Artworks owned by the villa’s owners
“Provide a complete historical overview of the owners of Villa Carlotta (
, Lake Como). Include dates of ownership and their influence on the villa.”
collection were located in Villa Carlotta.”
“Provide a complete list of artworks connected to Villa Carlotta through its owners.”
, and Villa Carlotta historically the same building? Provide historical evidence.”
Using four LLM systems and three prompting strategies, we successfully reconstructed
all missing historical, artistic, and landscape information
about Villa Carlotta that is absent in
. This information forms the basis for
unify the villa’s three historical names
This demonstrates how LLMs can meaningfully enhance incomplete knowledge graphs in the cultural heritage domain
